subject: Social Science - Geography
overall_learning_objectives: Explain the concept and characteristics of resources
Classify resources into natural, human-made, and human resources
Analyze renewable and non-renewable resources with examples
Understand the importance of resource conservation
Explain the concept of sustainable development
Identify the uneven distribution of resources across the world
Evaluate the role of humans in managing and conserving resources

lesson_plan.title: Understanding the Concept of Resources
topics_covered: Utility, value, and importance of resources
Examples of resources in everyday life
teaching_strategy: Brainstorming activity on resources used at home
student_activities: List resources used in daily life
Explain why these items are considered resources

lesson_plan.title: Classification of Resources
student_activities: Create a classification chart in notebooks

lesson_plan.title: Natural Resources and Their Uses
student_activities: Identify natural resources in the local environment
Discuss how these resources support livelihoods

lesson_plan.title: Renewable and Non-Renewable Resources
student_activities: Prepare a table comparing renewable and non-renewable resources
Discuss why non-renewable resources must be conserved

lesson_plan.title: Human-Made Resources and Human Resources
topics_covered: Importance of education in developing human resources
teaching_strategy: Discussion on skills and workforce development
student_activities: Identify examples of human-made resources in the community
Discuss how education improves human resources

lesson_plan.title: Resource Conservation and Sustainable Development
student_activities: Write three methods to conserve resources

lesson_plan.title: Responsible Resource Use and Chapter Revision
student_activities: List major concepts learned in the chapter

teacher_tip: Encourage students to connect concepts with real-life examples such as water usage, electricity consumption, and local environmental issues to help them understand the importance of resource conservation.
